text: exampleco builds a compliant draft roster from demand, availability and budget for a manager to review before publishing.
guardrail: Capability claim; state it builds a draft for review, not auto-publish. (legacy-shaped: no role tag — proves backward compatibility.)

text: Live wage cost against budget — wage % and sales per labour hour — updates as a roster is built, so labour spend is controlled before publish.
guardrail: Do not attach a labour-cost-saving percentage.

resonance.note: Operator-noted: lands with finance buyers in multi-site hospitality. Human-write-only — populated from the outcome ledger by a person, never auto-inferred.

pattern: We already have payroll software, so we don't need this.
response: exampleco sits in front of payroll, not on top of it: it captures award-interpreted hours and feeds them in, so the existing payroll system keeps running but stops being fed by spreadsheets. Position as complement, not replacement.
guardrail: Differentiation framing only; never disparage the prospect's named payroll vendor.

text: Multi-site operators cannot see live labour cost per venue until after the pay run, so overspend is only visible in hindsight.
guardrail: A pain hypothesis to probe in discovery, not a stated fact about the specific prospect.

differentiation: exampleco interprets Australian Modern Awards with exampleco-maintained Managed Templates that update automatically when minimum wages change — positioned as built-in award automation rather than a configure-it-yourself rules engine.
guardrail: State exampleco's differentiation only; do NOT assert what the competitor does or does not do — that is a claim about them, not us.
